# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.

Prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# ARM Template for Azure Key Vault with IAM setup in YAML format without any explanations or markdowns
parameters:
  keyVaultName: '<your-keyvault-name>' # replace this value by your actual name. Required parameter to be set before running the template via azure cli command line tool, e.g., --set parameters::myKeyVaultName="MyKeyVaultValue".
  location: '<location of key vault>'   # required if you want a specific resource group and region for your Key Vault (e.g.: westus) - optional when using default settings in Azure Cloud Shell or local development environment, e.g., --set parameters::myLocation="westcentralus".
  applicationClientId: '<application client id>' # required if you want to allow multiple applications and service principals read secrets (e.g.: "12345678-1234-1234-1234-1234567890ab").
  applicationSecret: '<application secret>' # required if you want to allow multiple applications and service principals read secrets (e.g.: "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ==") - optional when using default settings in Azure Cloud Shell or local development environment, e.g., --set parameters::myApplicationSecret="MyAppSecValue".
  roleName: '<role name>' # required if you want to allow multiple applications and service principals read secrets (e.g.: "Reader"). - optional when using default settings in Azure Cloud Shell or local development environment, e.g., --set parameters::myRole="Reader", then use this parameter with the value of '<role name>'
  roleAssignmentName: '<unique id for assignment rule (e.g.: "MyPolicyRule") >' # required if you want to allow multiple applications and service principals read secrets - optional when using default settings in Azure Cloud Shell or local development environment, e.g., --set parameters::myRoleAssignement="Reader", then use this parameter with the value of '<unique id for assignment rule>'.
